data: Due to Sun Valley's unpredictable winter weather conditions which sometimes necessitate diversions to alternate airports and use of ground transportation, Alaska Airlines will not be accepting unaccompanied minors for travel to and from Sun Valley between December 1 and April 1. This action is being taken specifically to ensure the safety and well-being of children traveling alone.

Children under the age of 13 must travel with someone aged 18 or over on select international flights. If you're booking a flight for someone 12 and under, please review our Unaccompanied Minor policies.
use points, if selected, nearby airports cannot be included

17 years old and under. Restrictions apply if traveling without an adult.

Each infant must have their own ticket when traveling with our international partners. We've added your infant as an adult passenger; you'll be able to enter their correct birthdate later, on the Passenger Information page.

This trip can't be booked online, but our Customer Care team is ready to assist you. Please call <planbook-hyperlink href="tel:+18002527522">1-800-252-7522</planbook-hyperlink> to complete your booking.

Discounts are not available when using points.
About credits, discounts, and companion fare codes
Credit and gift certificates are NOT discount codes. Enter credit and gift certificate codes on the Payment page with their associated PIN.

Discount codes are used to reduce the fare of applicable new ticket purchases and can be redeemed during their offer period at alaskaair.com, hawaiianairlines.com, the AlaskaHawaiian mobile app, and Atmos for Businessâ„¢. Discount codes cannot be redeemed after the offer period expires and are forfeited if the ticket is changed or canceled.

Companion fare codes are unique discount codes that are issued annually to eligible credit cardholders. They cannot be transferred or redeemed by anyone other than the cardholder and expire if not used within a year of issuance.

Flight Pass credits are unique discount codes that are issued on a monthly or bi-monthly basis depending on subscription plan enrollment. They cannot be transferred or redeemed by anyone other than the Flight Pass subscriber. Flight Pass credits expire if not used by the end of validity and are forfeited if the ticket is changed or canceled after validity.

Discount codes may be included in your Atmos Rewardsâ„¢ correspondence, social media, e-mail, direct mail, special advertisements, marketing campaigns, or via promotional partners. Some discount codes, such as companion fare codes which are issued to eligible cardholders, are stored in your account.
To redeem a discount code, type the code in the box marked discount code.
Discount codes can be stored in your account to be accessed for later use.
Only one discount code may be applied per reservation.
Discount codes may be used in conjunction with gift certificates or credit certificates, or Wallet funds.

Discount codes can only be redeemed for airfare purchases at alaskaair.com, hawaiianairlines.com, the AlaskaHawaiian app, and Atmos for Businessâ„¢ for electronic tickets with a maximum of eight segments.
Discount codes cannot be applied to Atmos Rewards award reservations.
Discount codes apply to original purchases only and cannot be applied during ticket exchanges. If the ticket is canceled, the value of the discount is forfeited.
Discount codes can only be redeemed for flights operated by Alaska Air, Hawaiian Airlines, Horizon Air, and/or Skywest as Alaska Airlines.
*Point-to-point fares are calculated for each flight's departure and arrival. Through fares are calculated from departure city to arrival city.

At this time, multi-city travel cannot be booked online for children aged 17 and under who are traveling without an adult. To book multi-city travel for children traveling alone, please contact Alaska Airlines Reservations call center at 1-800-ALASKAAIR (1-800-252-7522).
Upgrade certificates are not combinable with award itineraries.

While there is no minimum age for infants traveling with us, check with your doctor if you're unsure your infant should be traveling via plane. As of their second birthday, children are no longer considered infants. The Federal Aviation Administration requires all children who have reached their second birthday to occupy their own seat and travel on their own ticket. Alaska Airlines reserves the right to require proof of age for any child passenger. Please be prepared to provide a birth certificate, passport, or other documentation upon request.

Each ticketed adult may travel with one lap infant. To add a lap infant request to your reservation, call Alaska Airlines reservations at 1-800-252-7522 with your confirmation code. Because of the location of extra oxygen masks:
Alaska Airlines flights 1-1999 - only one lap infant is permitted per row (including both sides of aisle)
Alaska Airlines flights operated by Horizon Air 2000-2999 - one lap infant on each side of every row.
Alaska Airlines flights operated by SkyWest 3300-3499 - one lap infant on each side in every row.
Lap infants are not permitted in the emergency exit rows, any on Alaska Airlines flights.
Lap infants are not guaranteed a seat, but if there is an empty seat available on a flight, the infant may occupy that seat for no extra charge.
When traveling with a lap infant, a diaper bag will count toward the standard carry-on limit of the ticketed adult passenger. Lap infants are not entitled to their own carry-on allowance.
Lap infants in the First Class cabin are not eligible for complimentary meals.

Guests traveling with a lap infant on one of our partner airlines booked on alaskaair.com or via reservations must purchase an adult fare ticket for their lap infant (the infant will occupy a seat). All travelers, regardless of age, must purchase an adult fare/award ticket and will receive a seat and their own baggage allowance when applicable.

Lap infants travel free of charge within the United States, but are subject to select taxes and fees when traveling from an international location into the United States. Amounts below are listed in USD and rounded up to the nearest dollar. Fees may fluctuate based on currency exchange.
*Belizean citizens and children under the age of 12 may be eligible for Belize tourism tax refunds.
Travel from Costa Rica - approximately $50 USD

We will transport your child's car seat and stroller free of charge as checked baggage. You can check these items with your other baggage, or wait until you reach the gate area. This also applies to stroller-wagons that are designed to carry children and are collapsible to under 90 linear inches and 35 pounds. Standard bag fees/rules apply to larger wagons, those not designed to carry children, or those carried in addition to a stroller. Visit our batteries and electronics page if the car seat, stroller, or wagon has a battery or power bank.
